task_completion.description: Open NASA Panoply and load the precipitation dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NASA Panoply 'Sources' window is open, showing the loaded dataset (e.g., prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc) and the 'prate' (Precipitation Rate) variable is visible in the list.

task_completion.description: Create a 1D latitudinal profile plot for January at 20°E.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window showing a 1D line plot. The GUI controls must show Longitude set to 20°E (or 20) and Time set to January (or time index 1). The plot should display Latitude on the X-axis and Precipitation Rate on the Y-axis.

task_completion.description: Create a 1D latitudinal profile plot for July at 20°E.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window showing a 1D line plot. The GUI controls must show Longitude set to 20°E (or 20) and Time set to July (or time index 7). The plot should display Latitude on the X-axis and Precipitation Rate on the Y-axis.

task_completion.description: Export the generated profiles as PNG files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or 'Export' dialog in Panoply saving the plots with a .png extension, or a file manager/terminal window showing the newly created .png files (e.g., itcz_jan_profile.png, itcz_jul_profile.png) in the Documents directory.

task_completion.description: Save a report identifying the precise latitudes of peak precipitation for both months.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of the ITCZ report. The text must explicitly state the peak precipitation latitudes at 20°E: for January, a value between -15.0 and -5.0; for July, a value between 5.0 and 15.0.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the NASA Panoply GUI to generate the plots.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Panoply Java application interface being used. The agent should not use a Python script (e.g., matplotlib/xarray) in a terminal to bypass the Panoply requirement.

integrity.description: Ensure the plots reflect the requested parameters.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the Panoply plot window controls (Array 1 tab) to confirm that Longitude is genuinely set to 20E and the correct time steps (Jan/Jul) are selected, rather than the agent just changing the plot title.

integrity.description: Verify the report was created by the agent based on the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should be seen typing or echoing the report contents into a text file (e.g., using nano, gedit, or terminal redirects). The values should match the data analysis and not appear out of nowhere.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires analyzing the 'prate' variable from the provided netCDF file. The true peak precipitation latitude at longitude 20E in January is approximately -14.29 degrees (acceptable range: -15.0 to -5.0). In July, the peak is approximately 8.57 degrees (acceptable range: 5.0 to 15.0). The expected output files are the January and July PNG profiles and a text report containing these peak values.
